hooks.name: block-force-push-main
hooks.description: STORY 1: Block force push to main/master branches to prevent catastrophic history rewrites

action.message:
⛔ BLOCKED: Force push detected.

Force pushing to main/master can overwrite team members' work and break CI/CD.

If you absolutely need to force push:
1. Verify you're NOT on main/master branch
2. Communicate with your team
3. Use --force-with-lease (safer than --force)

This hook prevents accidental force pushes. Remove --force flag to proceed with normal push.

hooks.name: block-vendor-directory-writes
hooks.description: STORY 1: Block writes to vendor directories (dependencies should be managed by package managers)

action.message:
⛔ BLOCKED: Attempting to write to vendor/dependency directory.

Vendor directories (vendor/, node_modules/) contain managed dependencies and should not be manually edited.

Instead:
- For Go: Modify go.mod and run 'go mod vendor'
- For Node: Modify package.json and run 'npm install' or 'yarn install'

Manual edits to vendor directories will be overwritten on the next dependency update.

hooks.name: block-git-hook-skipping
hooks.description: STORY 1: Block git commands that skip hooks (--no-verify, --no-gpg-sign) to ensure safety checks run

action.message:
⛔ BLOCKED: Git command attempting to skip hooks.

Flags detected: --no-verify or --no-gpg-sign

Git hooks provide important safety checks:
- Pre-commit: Linting, formatting, tests
- Commit-msg: Commit message validation
- Pre-push: Final safety checks

Skipping hooks can introduce bugs, formatting issues, or bypass security checks.

If hooks are failing:
1. Fix the underlying issue (linting errors, test failures)
2. Update the hook if it's incorrectly blocking valid work
3. Never bypass hooks to 'save time'

Remove the --no-verify or --no-gpg-sign flag to proceed.

hooks.name: auto-format-typescript-javascript
hooks.description: STORY 2: Auto-format TypeScript/JavaScript/JSX files with Prettier after Write operations (Slabstack repo only)

action.command: FILE="{{file_path}}"; if command -v npx >/dev/null 2>&1 && command -v prettier >/dev/null 2>&1; then npx prettier --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null && echo "✓ Auto-formatted with Prettier: $FILE" || echo "⚠ Prettier formatting failed (file may not be valid JS/TS)"; else echo "⚠ Prettier not found. Install with: npm install -g prettier"; fi

hooks.name: auto-format-go
hooks.description: STORY 2: Auto-format Go files with gofmt after Write operations (Slabstack repo only)

action.command: FILE="{{file_path}}"; if command -v gofmt >/dev/null 2>&1; then gofmt -w "$FILE" 2>/dev/null && echo "✓ Auto-formatted with gofmt: $FILE" || echo "⚠ gofmt formatting failed (file may not be valid Go)"; else echo "⚠ gofmt not found. Install Go toolchain: https://golang.org/doc/install"; fi

hooks.name: warn-auto-generated-migrations
hooks.description: STORY 8: Warn when modifying auto-generated migration files (Slabstack repo only)

action.message:
⚠️  WARNING: Modifying database migration file.

Migration files can affect:
- Critical aggregation queries (product_agg_cte, actuals calculations)
- Downstream consumers (dashboards, reports, mobile app)
- Production data integrity

Before proceeding:
1. Have you read .claude/docs/blast-radius-guide.md?
2. Have you identified all queries that use this table?
3. Have you checked aggregation views that depend on this schema?

Press Enter to proceed or Ctrl+C to cancel and run /blast-radius first.

hooks.name: warn-projects-go-edits
hooks.description: STORY 8: Warn when modifying projects.go (contains product_agg_cte critical query) (Slabstack repo only)

action.message:
⚠️  HIGH-RISK FILE: projects.go

This file contains product_agg_cte, a critical financial aggregation query used across:
- Dashboard metrics
- Revenue reporting
- Customer billing
- Mobile app calculations

Changes to this file have caused production incidents in the past.

Before proceeding:
1. Have you completed blast radius analysis?
2. Have you tested with production-like data volumes?
3. Have you verified all downstream consumers still work?

Press Enter to proceed with extreme caution or Ctrl+C to cancel.

hooks.name: notify-long-bash-operations
hooks.description: STORY 9: Send desktop notification when bash commands exceed 30 seconds

action.command: DURATION_SEC=$(({{duration_ms}} / 1000)); COMMAND="{{command}}"; COMMAND_SHORT="${COMMAND:0:50}"; if command -v notify-send >/dev/null 2>&1; then notify-send -u normal -t 5000 "Claude Code - Operation Complete" "Long operation finished in ${DURATION_SEC}s:\n${COMMAND_SHORT}..."; echo "✓ Desktop notification sent (${DURATION_SEC}s elapsed)"; else echo "⚠ notify-send not installed. Install with: sudo apt install libnotify-bin"; fi
